description: Oracle test facts for knot invariants and their behavior under Reidemeister moves. Tests knowledge of Jones polynomial, writhe, bracket polynomial, and topological invariance.

facts.context: The Reidemeister R1 move (adding or removing a twist/kink) changes the writhe by:
facts.truth: ±1 (writhe is NOT a knot invariant because it changes under R1)
distractors: the writhe is undefined after R1

facts.context: The Reidemeister R2 and R3 moves change the writhe by:
facts.truth: 0 (R2 and R3 preserve writhe, only R1 changes it)

facts.context: The Kauffman bracket polynomial <K> under R1 (adding a positive kink) transforms as:
facts.truth: <K with kink> = -A^{-3} <K> (bracket is NOT invariant under R1)

facts.context: The Kauffman bracket polynomial <K> under R2 and R3 moves:
facts.truth: is invariant (bracket is preserved under R2 and R3, but not R1)
distractors: changes by a factor of A

facts.context: The Jones polynomial V(K) under all three Reidemeister moves R1, R2, R3:
facts.truth: is invariant (Jones polynomial is a true knot invariant)
distractors: is only defined for alternating knots

facts.context: The Jones polynomial is related to the Kauffman bracket by:
facts.truth: V(K) = (-A^3)^{-writhe} × <K>, where the normalization cancels R1 changes

facts.context: The Jones polynomial is invariant under R1 because:
facts.truth: R1 changes writhe by ±1 and bracket by -A^{∓3}, which cancel in the normalized formula
distractors: R1 doesn't affect the polynomial at all

facts.context: The Jones polynomial of the unknot (trivial knot) is:
facts.truth: V(unknot) = 1 (the unknot has the simplest Jones polynomial)

facts.context: The Jones polynomial of the right-handed trefoil knot is:
facts.truth: V(t) = t + t³ - t⁴ (distinguishes trefoil from unknot)

facts.context: The HOMFLY-PT polynomial P(a,z) is related to the Jones polynomial by:
facts.truth: Jones is a specialization: V(t) = P(t, t^{1/2} - t^{-1/2})
distractors: HOMFLY is not a knot invariant

facts.context: The Jones polynomial satisfies the skein relation:
The crossing number c(K) of a knot K is:
facts.truth: the minimum number of crossings over all diagrams of K (an invariant)
distractors: the number of crossings in any diagram

facts.truth: the minimum number of crossing changes needed to convert K to the unknot

facts.context: For a two-component link L = L1 ∪ L2, the linking number lk(L1, L2) is:
facts.truth: half the sum of crossing signs between components (invariant under all R-moves)

facts.context: The figure-eight knot (4_1) has the special property of being:
facts.truth: amphichiral (equivalent to its mirror image, same Jones polynomial as mirror)
the trefoil from its mirror image (left vs right-handed), and many knots from unknot
